- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Arkoma Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/arkoma-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): Hughes County (OK) has the highest daily oil production at 36 barrels per day (bo/d), with 58 wells. This makes it the most productive among the listed counties, followed by Coal County with 18.1 bo/d and 28 wells.
- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Powder River Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/powder-river-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): Converse County has the highest daily oil production at 95,734 barrels per day (bo/d) across 536 wells. Campbell County follows with 45,145 bo/d from 377 wells. These counties lead oil output in the listed dataset by a significant margin.
- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Delaware Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/delaware-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): Lea County leads daily oil production with 1,018,333.9 barrels per day (bo/d) from 4,111 wells. Eddy County ranks second with 789,231.2 bo/d from 3,295 wells. These two counties dominate oil output among the listed regions.
- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Central Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/central-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): Andrews County has the highest daily oil production at 14,979.2 barrels per day (bo/d) from 113 wells. Crane County follows with 12,522.3 bo/d from 109 wells. These counties lead production in the listed dataset.
- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Midland Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/midland-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): Midland Basin’s Oil Production Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Midland Basin Martin County leads Texas oil production with 596,000 barrels...
- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Barnett Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/barnett-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): Barnett Basin’s Oil Production Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Barnett Basin Denton County leads North Texas oil production with 513....
- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Denver Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/denver-julesburg-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): Weld County dominates oil production in the Rockies region with 298,502 barrels per day from 2,819 wells. Adams and Arapahoe counties follow with significant output. Smaller producers like Broomfield, Larimer, and Jackson contribute modest volumes. This data provides a snapshot of regional activity for operators and investors monitoring Colorado and surrounding states.
- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Uinta Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/uinta-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): Duchesne County leads Utah oil production with 95,077 barrels per day from 427 wells. Uintah and Rio Blanco follow with lower volumes. Carbon County reports zero production from one well. This snapshot highlights northeastern Utah’s oil activity for regional operators and analysts.
- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Anadarko Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/anadarko-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): Anadarko Basin’s Oil Production Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Anadarko Basin Canadian County leads oil production in Oklahoma’s Panhandle with...
- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Appalachia Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/appalachia-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): Carroll County leads Appalachian oil production with 28,525 barrels per day from 107 wells. Guernsey and Harrison follow closely. Wetzel, Marshall, and Washington contribute moderate output. Counties like Bradford, Lycoming, and Susquehanna report zero production despite high well counts. This snapshot highlights oil trends across Ohio, Pennsylvania, and West Virginia.

- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Permian Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/permian-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): The top oil-producing county in the Permian Basin for 2025 is Lea County, Martin, and Midland counties follow. These counties lead due to high well counts and sustained output from advanced horizontal drilling techniques.
- [Top Gas-Producing Counties in the Permian Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/permian-basin/top-gas-counties-2025/): The top gas-producing counties in the Permian Basin for 2025 are Reeves, Loving, and Lea. Reeves County leads in natural gas output, followed by Loving in Texas and Lea in New Mexico. Production trends show increased output from horizontal drilling, with growth concentrated in the Delaware sub-basin.
- [Top Gas-Producing Counties in the Eagle Ford Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/eagle-ford-basin/top-gas-counties-2025/): The top gas-producing county in the Eagle Ford shale for 2025 is Webb County, with daily production of 2,122,846 Mcf. Karnes, La Salle, and DeWitt counties follow, each producing over 500,000 Mcf per day. High production levels are driven by favorable geology and well productivity.
- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the Eagle Ford Basin (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/eagle-ford-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/): The top oil-producing county in the Eagle Ford shale for 2025 is Karnes County, with daily production of 176,052.1 barrels. DeWitt, La Salle, and McMullen counties follow in rank. High oil output in these counties correlates with strong well counts and mature drilling infrastructure.

- [Top Oil-Producing Counties in the U.S. (2025 Update)](https://novilabs.com/top-oil-counties-us/): Lea County, New Mexico, produces the most daily oil in the U.S. with 1,028,137.7 barrels per day. It leads all counties in both well count (4,200) and total oil output, located in the prolific Permian Basin.

## Data types

- [Rig and Frac Crew Data | Oil & Gas Upstream Data](https://novilabs.com/well-data/rig-and-frac-crew/): Novi’s Upstream Data Rig and Frac Crew Data Tracking the movement and activity of drilling rigs and frac crews is...
- [Production Data](https://novilabs.com/well-data/production-data/): Novi’s Upstream Data US Well Production Data Novi Labs provides highly accurate and comprehensive oil & gas production datasets, surpassing...
- [Acreage Maps](https://novilabs.com/well-data/acreage-maps/): Novi’s Upstream Data Acreage Maps In the competitive landscape of North American oil and gas, acreage maps are the first...
- [Lease-to-Well Production Allocation](https://novilabs.com/well-data/well-production-allocation/): Novi’s Upstream Data Lease-to-Well Production Allocation To conduct comprehensive well-level analysis, it is crucial to break down production data to...

- Published: 2025-08-12
- Modified: 2025-10-28
- URL: https://novilabs.com/the-ultimate-guide-to-oil-production-forecasting-methods-and-analysis-2025/
---

> Hughes County (OK) has the highest daily oil production at 36 barrels per day (bo/d), with 58 wells. This makes it the most productive among the listed counties, followed by Coal County with 18.1 bo/d and 28 wells.

- Published: 2025-08-07
- Modified: 2025-08-07
- URL: https://novilabs.com/arkoma-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

> Converse County has the highest daily oil production at 95,734 barrels per day (bo/d) across 536 wells. Campbell County follows with 45,145 bo/d from 377 wells. These counties lead oil output in the listed dataset by a significant margin.

- Published: 2025-08-07
- Modified: 2025-08-07
- URL: https://novilabs.com/powder-river-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

> Lea County leads daily oil production with 1,018,333.9 barrels per day (bo/d) from 4,111 wells. Eddy County ranks second with 789,231.2 bo/d from 3,295 wells. These two counties dominate oil output among the listed regions.

- Published: 2025-08-07
- Modified: 2025-08-07
- URL: https://novilabs.com/delaware-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

> Andrews County has the highest daily oil production at 14,979.2 barrels per day (bo/d) from 113 wells. Crane County follows with 12,522.3 bo/d from 109 wells. These counties lead production in the listed dataset.

- Published: 2025-08-07
- Modified: 2025-08-07
- URL: https://novilabs.com/central-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

- Published: 2025-08-06
- Modified: 2025-11-14
- URL: https://novilabs.com/midland-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

- Published: 2025-08-06
- Modified: 2025-09-29
- URL: https://novilabs.com/barnett-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

> Weld County dominates oil production in the Rockies region with 298,502 barrels per day from 2,819 wells. Adams and Arapahoe counties follow with significant output. Smaller producers like Broomfield, Larimer, and Jackson contribute modest volumes. This data provides a snapshot of regional activity for operators and investors monitoring Colorado and surrounding states.

- Published: 2025-08-06
- Modified: 2025-08-06
- URL: https://novilabs.com/denver-julesburg-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

> Duchesne County leads Utah oil production with 95,077 barrels per day from 427 wells. Uintah and Rio Blanco follow with lower volumes. Carbon County reports zero production from one well. This snapshot highlights northeastern Utah’s oil activity for regional operators and analysts.

- Published: 2025-08-06
- Modified: 2025-08-06
- URL: https://novilabs.com/uinta-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

- Published: 2025-08-06
- Modified: 2025-09-01
- URL: https://novilabs.com/anadarko-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

> Carroll County leads Appalachian oil production with 28,525 barrels per day from 107 wells. Guernsey and Harrison follow closely. Wetzel, Marshall, and Washington contribute moderate output. Counties like Bradford, Lycoming, and Susquehanna report zero production despite high well counts. This snapshot highlights oil trends across Ohio, Pennsylvania, and West Virginia.

- Published: 2025-08-06
- Modified: 2025-08-06
- URL: https://novilabs.com/appalachia-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

- Published: 2025-06-30
- Modified: 2025-10-02
- URL: https://novilabs.com/faq/
---

> The top oil-producing county in the Permian Basin for 2025 is Lea County, Martin, and Midland counties follow. These counties lead due to high well counts and sustained output from advanced horizontal drilling techniques.

- Published: 2025-06-30
- Modified: 2025-06-30
- URL: https://novilabs.com/permian-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

> The top gas-producing counties in the Permian Basin for 2025 are Reeves, Loving, and Lea. Reeves County leads in natural gas output, followed by Loving in Texas and Lea in New Mexico. Production trends show increased output from horizontal drilling, with growth concentrated in the Delaware sub-basin.

- Published: 2025-06-30
- Modified: 2025-06-30
- URL: https://novilabs.com/permian-basin/top-gas-counties-2025/
---

> The top gas-producing county in the Eagle Ford shale for 2025 is Webb County, with daily production of 2,122,846 Mcf. Karnes, La Salle, and DeWitt counties follow, each producing over 500,000 Mcf per day. High production levels are driven by favorable geology and well productivity.

- Published: 2025-06-30
- Modified: 2025-06-30
- URL: https://novilabs.com/eagle-ford-basin/top-gas-counties-2025/
---

> The top oil-producing county in the Eagle Ford shale for 2025 is Karnes County, with daily production of 176,052.1 barrels. DeWitt, La Salle, and McMullen counties follow in rank. High oil output in these counties correlates with strong well counts and mature drilling infrastructure.

- Published: 2025-06-30
- Modified: 2025-08-07
- URL: https://novilabs.com/eagle-ford-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

- Published: 2025-06-30
- Modified: 2025-11-14
- URL: https://novilabs.com/haynesville-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

- Published: 2025-06-30
- Modified: 2025-11-14
- URL: https://novilabs.com/haynesville-basin/top-gas-counties-2025/
---

- Published: 2025-06-30
- Modified: 2025-09-29
- URL: https://novilabs.com/bakken-basin/top-gas-counties-2025/
---

- Published: 2025-06-30
- Modified: 2025-09-29
- URL: https://novilabs.com/bakken-basin/top-oil-counties-2025/
---

> Lea County, New Mexico, produces the most daily oil in the U.S. with 1,028,137.7 barrels per day. It leads all counties in both well count (4,200) and total oil output, located in the prolific Permian Basin.

- Published: 2025-06-30
- Modified: 2025-08-07
- URL: https://novilabs.com/top-oil-counties-us/

- Authors: Enno Peters
Permian tight oil production was flat at about 4.4 million b/d in December (after upcoming revisions, horizontal wells only). In 2021, some 4,200 horizontal wells were completed, about 20% more than in the year prior, although still well below the over 5,000 wells coming online in both 2018 & 2019.

---

- Published: 2022-03-18
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-january-2022/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota from horizontal wells fell by 5% m-o-m, to 1.04 million b/d in January. Natural gas output dropped by the 7% to below 2.8 Bcf/d.

- Modified: 2026-03-05

- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
US tight oil production rose to almost 7.6 million b/d in October (after upcoming revisions), 0.4 million b/d higher than 12 months earlier. Tight gas continued to rise as well, and came in at 72 Bcf/d in October (excluding Ohio & West Virginia and after upcoming revisions).

- Published: 2022-03-02
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/overcoming-barriers-to-data-driven-workflows-introducing-novi-model-engine/
- Categories: Novi Product Update
- Authors: Ted Cross
Although shiny machine learning algorithms get most of the attention, data preparation takes the most work. Erroneous completions data must be fixed or removed. Messy formation names need to be cleaned up.

- Authors: Enno Peters
Permian tight oil production continues to recover and in September topped 4.2 million b/d (after upcoming revisions, horizontal wells only). We expect that earlier in Q4 this year already a new output record was set. Natural gas production rose as well, and was close to 17 Bcf/d in September.

---

- Published: 2021-12-16
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-october-2021/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota from horizontal wells was flat m-o-m for the 2nd month in a row, at 1.07 million b/d in October. Natural gas output, at 2.9 Bcf/d, was close to the historical high in November 2019 (3.1 Bcf/d). Only 492 new horizontal wells were completed through October, the lowest number since 2009.

---

- Published: 2021-12-14
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-august-2021/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
US tight oil production rose to over 7.3 million b/d in August (after upcoming revisions), the highest level since May last year (6.2 million b/d), although still below the peak in November 2019 (8.4 million b/d). Excluding West Virginia, tight gas production is already setting new records, and was 78 Bcf/d in August (toggle product to 'Gas' to see this).

Tight oil production in the Eagle Ford was flat for the 5th consecutive month in August, at 1.1 million b/d (Hz. wells only, after upcoming revisions). Natural gas production came in at 5.7 Bcf/d, a level at which it has also hovered around now for several months.

---

- Published: 2021-11-17
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-august-2021/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Permian tight oil production is slowly accelerating and in August rose to about 4.2 million b/d (after upcoming revisions, horizontal wells only), the highest since April 2020 and less than 300 thousand b/d below the peak in March 2020. Natural gas production is already setting new records every month, and came in at 16.6 Bcf/d in August.

---

- Published: 2021-11-09
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-september-2021/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota from horizontal wells was flat m-o-m, at 1.07 million b/d in September. Output fell by 120 thousand b/d in the 12-month period through September, or 10%. But drilling activity has picked up, and we estimate that with the 23 rigs that are drilling horizontal wells in the state (according to Baker Hughes), current output can be maintained for a while.

---

- Published: 2021-11-04
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-july-2021/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
US tight oil production was flat m-o-m in July, at 7.2 million b/d (after upcoming revisions), almost 1.5 million b/d below the November 2019 peak. Natural gas output was also basically flat, at just above 70 Bcf/d (excluding Ohio & West Virginia).

---

- Published: 2021-11-02
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/haynesville-update-through-july-2021/
- Categories: Haynesville
- Authors: Enno Peters
Natural gas production in the Haynesville set a new record in July, at over 12 Bcf/d (after upcoming revisions). This constitutes a growth of over 50% compared with the peak at the end of 2011 and almost a tripling of natural gas production in less than 5 years. After the Permian, the Haynesville is where most of the rigs are that are drilling horizontal wells.

---

- Published: 2021-10-28
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/pennsylvania-update-through-august-2021/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Natural gas production in Pennsylvania rose with about 1% m-o-m in August, to 20.5 Bcf/d (Hz. wells only). The main reason behind the recent growth in output is that more wells were completed than were drilled. With just over 500 DUCs, the current drilled-but-uncompleted horizontal well count is the lowest in a decade.

---

- Modified: 2026-04-23

Oil production in North Dakota from horizontal wells increased by 2% to 1.06 million b/d in August. Only 366 horizontal wells came online this year through August, the lowest number in a decade.

- Published: 2021-10-05
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-june-2021/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
US tight oil production was flat m-o-m in June, at 7.3 million b/d (after upcoming revisions). Natural gas production was above 82 Bcf/d, bringing it on par with the record high in November 2019. In the first 6 months of this year, fewer than 4 thousand horizontal wells came online in all the US shale basins, the lowest number in a decade.

---

- Published: 2021-10-01
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/pennsylvania-update-through-july-2021/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Natural gas production in Pennsylvania was flat month over month in August, at 20.3 Bcf/d (hz. wells only). Despite nearly decade natural gas prices, the number of wells that came online through July (302) is the lowest in over a decade.

---

- Published: 2021-09-23
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-june-2021/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
Tight oil production in the Eagle Ford was flat for the 3rd consecutive month in June, at 1.1 million b/d (Hz. wells only, after upcoming revisions). The first time the Eagle Ford produced at this level was 8 years earlier, when WTI was almost at $100/bbl.

---

- Published: 2021-09-17
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-june-2021/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Tight oil production in the Permian rose by about 1% in June to about 4.1 million b/d in June (after revisions, horizontal wells only). Natural gas production held flat at above 16 Bcf/d.

---

- Published: 2021-09-10
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-july-2021/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota from horizontal wells fell m-o-m by 50 thousand bo/d in July (5%). Although 46 wells began producing in July, the same number as in June, their average initial performance was poorer than usual, which explains about half of the decline. Natural gas production fell as well, to 2.8 Bcf/d.

---

- Published: 2021-09-08
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-may-2021/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
US tight oil production rose by 100 thousand b/d in May, to 7.2 million b/d (after upcoming revisions). This was almost a million b/d higher than a year earlier, when extremely low prices caused a significant part of production to be curtailed. But compared with the peak in November 2019, output fell by almost 1.5 million b/d. Natural gas production (excl. Ohio & West Virginia) grew slightly as well, to a fresh record at just above 70 Bcf/d.

---

- Published: 2021-09-01
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/pennsylvania-update-through-june-2021/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Natural gas production in Pennsylvania was flat month over month in June, at 20.3 Bcf/d (hz. wells only). In the first 6 months of this year, 247 horizontal wells were completed, vs. 273 in the same period last year.

---

- Published: 2021-08-24
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-may-2021/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
Tight oil production in the Eagle Ford was flat for the 2nd consecutive month in May, at 1.1 million b/d (after upcoming revisions); a loss of almost 0.7 million b/d since the peak in March 2015. Natural gas production, which has lost far less ground since the peak in October 2015, came in at 5.8 Bcf/d in May.

Oil production in North Dakota from horizontal wells came in at 1.09 million bo/d in June, unchanged from the month before. Natural gas production was also steady at 2.9 Bcf/d. In the first half of this year, 271 horizontal wells were completed, versus 396 in 2020 (and 575 in 2019).

- Published: 2021-07-26

- Authors: Enno Peters

- Authors: Enno Peters

About half of it was produced by wells that were completed since early last year, as is visualized by the light & dark blue areas in the chart above. Natural gas production, at over 15 Bcf/d, was close to record levels.

- Published: 2021-07-09
- Modified: 2026-03-05

Oil production in North Dakota from horizontal wells rose by less than 1% in May, m-o-m, to 1.09 million bo/d. Natural gas production increased to 2.94 Bcf/d. Only 44 wells were completed in May, but the inactive well count fell by over 100 to just below 1,500.

---

- Published: 2021-07-07
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-march-2021/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
March US tight oil production more than made up for the 1 million b/d loss in February, and at 7.1 million b/d was slightly higher than the January level. Natural gas production (excl. West Virginia) also recovered by 6 Bcf/d, and was at 73.6 Bcf/d just 3% below the historical peak in November 2019 (76.1 Bcf/d).

---

- Published: 2021-06-30
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/haynesville-update-through-march-2021/
- Categories: Haynesville
- Authors: Enno Peters
Natural gas production in the Haynesville grew by almost 1.5 Bcf/d in March, more than recovering from the severe drop in the previous month. A fresh record was set at 11.5 Bcf/d, as we predicted in the previous post on this basin. Well productivity is at record levels (see "Well quality").

---

- Published: 2021-06-23
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/pennsylvania-update-through-april-2021/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Natural gas production in Pennsylvania fell by 1% in April, to 20.3 Bcf/d (hz. wells only). Only 160 new horizontal wells were completed through April this year, versus 180 and 206 in the same period in 2020 & 2019, respectively.

- Published: 2021-06-17
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-march-2021/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Tight oil production in the Permian rose by 800 thousand bo/d in March (horizontal wells only), more than compensating the fall in the preceding month due to the cold weather. At 3.9 million bo/d, output was the highest since April last year.

---

- Published: 2021-06-10
- Modified: 2026-03-05

Oil production in North Dakota from horizontal wells rose by 1% in April, m-o-m, to 1.08 million bo/d. Natural gas production has hovered just below 3.0 Bcf/d since October. Only 177 horizontal wells began production in the first 4 months of this year, the lowest in over a decade.

---

- Published: 2021-06-03
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-february-2021/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
US tight oil production fell by 1 million b/d to 6 million b/d in February, the lowest level in 3 years, as cold winter weather affected production in several states. This was just a blip, and based on preliminary data we expect production to have fully recovered in March.

---

- Published: 2021-06-01
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/haynesville-update-through-february-2021/
- Categories: Haynesville
- Authors: Enno Peters
After setting a new record in January, natural gas production in the Haynesville fell by about 10% in February (after revisions), to just over 10 Bcf/d. The region was impacted by a brief period of arctic temperatures. However, activity levels are high here, and in March or April probably a new production record was set.

---

- Published: 2021-05-28
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/oil-and-gas-cube-development-how-dense-is-too-dense/
- Categories: Artificial Intelligence in Oil and Gas
- Authors: Ted Cross
---

- Published: 2021-05-24
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/pennsylvania-update-through-march-2021/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Natural gas production in Pennsylvania was flat in March at 20.6 Bcf/d (hz. wells only, after revisions). Year over year growth was just over 1 Bcf/d. With only 126 new horizontal wells completed through March, completion activity is at a decade low.

---

- Published: 2021-05-20
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-february-2021/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
Like its bigger cousin the Permian (see here), tight oil production in the Eagle Ford was badly affected by the winter freeze in February. Output fell by about 150 thousand bo/d, to below 900 thousand bo/d (hz. wells only), the lowest level in almost 8 years. Still, the downturn was short-lived and March production should be back up to above 1 million bo/d.

---

- Published: 2021-05-18
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-february-2021/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Tight oil production in the Permian fell by over 600 thousand bo/d in February, the largest single-month decline in its history. Operations were severely impacted by the coldest weather in 30 years. Production plunged to just above 3 million bo/d (hz. wells only), the lowest volume in 2.5 years. Based on preliminary production data, we expect that March output was back to the January level.

---

- Published: 2021-05-11
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-march-2021/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota from horizontal wells grew by 2% m-o-m, to 1.07 million bo/d in March, after a 6% drop the month before, when several states were hit by harsh winter weather. Natural gas production was with 2.9 Bcf/d not far from the peak exactly one year earlier, at 3.1 Bcf/d.

---

- Published: 2021-05-05
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-january-2021/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
US tight oil production fell by less than 1% in January m-o-m, to 6.9 million b/d (after upcoming revisions, hz. wells only). Tight gas output continued its upward trend since last May, although it is still below the March 2020 high (toggle Product to gas to see this).

---

- Published: 2021-04-30
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/pennsylvania-update-through-february-2021/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Natural gas production in Pennsylvania was flat in February (after upcoming revisions), at about 20.8 Bcf/d (hz. wells only), after setting a record in January. In the first 2 months of the year, only 84 horizontal wells began producing.

- Published: 2021-04-22
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/haynesville-update-through-january-2021/
- Categories: Haynesville
- Authors: Enno Peters
Natural gas production set a new record in the Haynesville in January, at about 11.5 Bcf/d (after upcoming revisions).

---

- Published: 2021-04-21
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-january-2021/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
Tight oil production in the Eagle Ford was flat m-o-m in January, at about 1.05 million bo/d (after upcoming revisions). Compared with a year earlier, output was down by 300 thousand barrels of oil.

---

- Published: 2021-04-15
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-january-2021/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Since June last year, Permian tight oil production has hovered at a level close to 3.8 million bo/d (after upcoming revisions, hz. wells only). Natural gas production is at record heights (>15 Bcf/d). In the next update on this basin, we should learn exactly how much output was impacted by the harsh weather in February.

---

- Published: 2021-04-11
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-february-2021/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota from horizontal wells fell by 6% m-o-m, to 1.05 million bo/d in February, based on preliminary data. Only 1 well came online every day in the first 2 months, on average, while more than 2 are required to sustain the current output.

- Published: 2021-04-08
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-december-2020/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
US tight oil production fell below 7.0 million bo/d in December, the first time in the 2nd half of last year. Fewer than 7,500 horizontal wells were completed within this 12 states in 2020, significantly lower than the 13 thousand that came online in each of the previous two years. The y-o-y decline in output was therefore large in December, at around 1.5 million bo/d, as you'll find in the graph above.

- Published: 2021-03-16
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/niobrara-co-wy-update-through-december-2020/
- Categories: Niobrara
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in the DJ-Niobrara fell by almost 1/3rd from 579 thousand b/d to 390 thousand b/d in the 13 months from the peak in November 2019 through December last year. The wells in this area have high decline rates and in 2020 fewer than half the number of wells were completed (588) than in the years before. December production was the lowest level in over 3 years.

- Published: 2021-02-15
- Modified: 2026-03-05

- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Permian tight oil production rose in November by 1% m-o-m to ~3.8 million bo/d (after upcoming revisions). Natural gas production is with 14.8 Bcf/d close to a record level and the basin is now about 25% gassier than 2 years ago, as fewer wells came online last year(new wells start with a low gas-to-oil ratio). I expect that this trend will continue as the basin continues to age, simply because the gas-to-oil ratio for older wells is even higher (~6 Mcf/bbl after 5 years on average).

- Published: 2021-01-12

Although more than 2,100 wells are still shut-in (up from 1,500 before the pandemic started and down from the 4,300 in May), I don't expect this number to fall by much anymore, as it hasn't over the last month. Through November 2020, 551 wells started production, which was less than half compared with the same period in the year before (1,176).

- Published: 2020-12-16
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/what-can-chess-teach-us-about-artificial-intelligence-in-oil-and-gas/
- Categories: Artificial Intelligence in Oil and Gas
- Authors: Ted Cross
If, like me, you haven’t seriously played chess since you were young, you may find the advancements in chess artificial intelligence (or chess “engines”) incredible. But can chess teach us about the future of artificial intelligence in oil and gas?  Chess has long been a focus of artificial intelligence research. The game offers both clear rules (to easily measure success) and sufficient complexity (with at least 10^120 possible moves, no computer will ever be able to “solve” the game, unlike checkers). We certainly have complexity in the oilfield – but we also have the ability to measure success in the form of barrels produced, feet of pay encountered, or time to process a seismic volume.  In this post, we'll walk through the latest AI in oil and gas market developments in chess and how they might impact the oilfield. Read the full post here.

---

At 3.2 million bo/d, they produce well below the level in this basin.

- Published: 2020-08-26
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/vista-record-analysis-machine-learning-oil-and-gas-data-model/
- Categories: Well Completions
- Authors: Ted Cross
After the Great Coronavirus Shut-In, Vista Oil & Gas turned back on their new Vaca Muerta pad. A pleasant surprise greeted them. Their oil and gas machine learning model showed the highest production the play had ever seen. Both the MDM-2063 and MDM-2061 wells produced over 2,000 bbl/d on average for the month, with peak daily rates breaching 3,000 boe/d.  Why did these wells produce so much? Was it a novel completion design, a geologic hot spot, or a heavy tailwind of flush production? Lets explore this oil and gas machine learning use case. We will put these wells under the microscope with our Vaca Muerta public-data model.

- Published: 2020-08-07
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/making-sense-of-it-all-extracting-actionable-core-data-from-pxrf-using-pca-and-k-means-cluster-analysis/
- Categories: Big Data Management
- Authors: Esben Pedersen
WHY CARE ABOUT PXRF DATA? Thanks to relatively short scanning times and low operating cost, portable X-ray Fluorescence (pXRF) scanning of geologic core samples has become a burgeoning big data in oil and gas industry, with the technique capable of generating high resolution datasets containing comprehensive major and trace element abundances present in the rock.  We will be using Scikit-learn, an open source machine learning library that supports supervised and unsupervised learning towards a variety of applications. Here we will be utilizing principal component analysis and k-means clustering algorithms to manipulate and segment the big data before visualization.  The process outlined in this blog post develops labeled datasets that can be tied to the core, ahead of the implementation of a neural network. Read the full post here.

---

- Modified: 2026-03-05

Of course the low, and even negative, oil prices in March and April were the main cause, which led many operators to shut in their oil wells.

- Published: 2020-07-14
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/geoshap-estimating-rock-quality-with-machine-learning-models/
- Categories: Big Data Management
- Authors: Ted Cross
Can a machine learning model learn where the play sweet spots are, just from raw well logs? The answer is YES. Using logs (or any input geo variables), plus a principal components analysis, gives the model everything it needs to learn what drives production.  This is the subject of our most requested URTeC 2020 paper -- geoSHAP: A Novel Method of Deriving Rock Quality Index from Machine Learning Models and Principal Components Analysis.  Click through to learn more!

---

- Published: 2020-07-13
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-may-2020/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
North Dakota oil production fell by 350 thousand bo/d in May to 0.85 million bo/d, an even larger decline than we saw in the previous month (200 kbo/d). The last time the state produced this much was 7 years ago.

---

- Published: 2020-07-08
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-march-2020/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production from these wells set another record in November, at over 7.6 million bo/d (after upcoming revisions). The over 10 thousand horizontal wells that came online in 2019 contributed half of this supply (3.8 million bo/d), as is visualized by the dark blue area in the graph above.

---

- Published: 2020-07-06
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/oil-well-water-analysis-forecast-produced-water-with-machine-learning/
- Categories: Big Data Management
- Authors: Ted Cross
How often does an engineer dash off a simple produced water analysis, doing something like applying a flat WOR to their oil prediction? It's easy to ignore water, but unexpectedly high production, leading to more produced water, can damage well economics. In the worst cases, it can force shut-in if disposal capacity is full.  Fortunately, advanced machine learning methods developed for oil can be applied to water. These technique help disentangle the complex interactions of completions, geology, and spacing.  This is the subject of our URTeC 2020 (and JPT-featured) paper. Read the summary here.

---

- Published: 2020-06-30
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-march-2020/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
March oil production hovered at around 1.34 million bo/d, the same level as since the start of the year (after upcoming revisions). As is shown by the colored areas in the chart above, over half of production in March came from wells that started producing since 2019.

---

- Published: 2020-06-26
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/smart-oil-well-design-completions-optimization-with-machine-learning/
- Categories: Well Completions
- Authors: Ted Cross
Using a single scaling factor instead of a time series can ruin your well economics. An upsized completion might increase your production 20%, but knowing whether that applies to peak rate or EUR can have a huge impact on your well economics.  Machine learning models can predict a time series of production. This means you can evaluate the impact of completions design over the life of a well. Read the URTeC paper summary here.

---

In March, about 4.1 million bo/d was produced from horizontal wells in the Permian (after upcoming revisions). The impact of the low prices only started to become visible in the following month, although, based on preliminary data (available in our subscription services), the drop appears to have been less than e.g. in North Dakota.

Natural gas production remained unchanged at 19.3 Bcf/d in April. Compared with the peak in November (19.7 Bcf/d), production was down by 2%. Only 180 new horizontal wells came online in the first 4 months this year, the lowest number in the past decade.

- Published: 2020-05-15

- Categories: Big Data Management

- Published: 2020-05-14
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/niobrara-co-wy-update-through-february-2020/
- Categories: Niobrara
- Authors: Enno Peters
These interactive presentations contain the latest oil & gas production data, from all 10,287 horizontal wells that started production in Colorado and Wyoming since 2009/2010, through January. Oil production in these 2 states started the year at record production (after revisions), at over 620 thousand bo/d. Both states contributed to the growth in the past 12 months; Colorado with ~20%, and Wyoming even at almost 50% (although from a lower base). Production in the Powder River Basin has been mostly responsible for the latter, and is now at over 120 thousand bo/d.

- Published: 2020-04-30

- Categories: Digital Oilfield Technology
- Authors: Ted Cross
Whether exploring for oil offshore Brazil or scaling type curves in Lea County, engineers and geologists rely upon the power of analogy to estimate the productivity of a given area or engineering design choice. Tree-based machine learning models can quantify similarity based on what matters: how it contributes to production.

- Published: 2019-10-28
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-july-2019/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Upward revisions for the summer months were relatively large in the production data that the RRC released last week. Based on the latest actual data, May production in the Permian (including New Mexico) was already at 3.5 million bo/d, which will increase further once all revisions are in. July, also shown here at 3.5 million bo/d, will probably be revised even more (as is common for more recent months), to around 3.7-3.8 million bo/d eventually.

---

- Published: 2019-10-21
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/pennsylvania-update-through-august-2019/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
After record gas production in July, August set a new high again at 18.6 Bcf/d. But completion activity is lower than last year; 473 wells came online in the first 8 months of this year, versus 525 in the same period last year.

---

- Published: 2019-10-14
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-august-2019/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil and gas production in North Dakota set new records in August, as both increased by more than 2% m-o-m. This is based on preliminary data, as the DMR's Director's Cut will be released on Thursday.

---

- Published: 2019-10-08
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-june-2019/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production from horizontal wells in these states increased slightly in June, to around 7 million bo/d (after upcoming revisions). That would represent a growth of 100 thousand bo/d in the first 6 months, far lower than the ~670 thousand bo/d increase in the same period last year, but still positive.

---

- Published: 2019-09-30
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-june-2019/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production came in at 1.3 million bo/d in June, unchanged from the month before. Since March, the horizontal rig count in this basin has dropped from 82 to 62, the lowest number in the past 1.5 year. Natural gas production was 6.2 Bcf/d, almost back to the level in December last year.

---

- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-june-2019/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
June production came in at just over 3.3 million bo/d, which after upcoming revisions will get close to 3.5 million bo/d. That would represent a growth of about 200 thousand bo/d in the first 6 months, which is significant, but well under the growth rate in the past 2 years.

- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/pennsylvania-update-through-july-2019/

- Authors: Enno Peters
Natural gas production from these horizontal wells stayed at 18.3 Bcf/d in June. Similar to previous years, almost no growth was seen in the first 6 months of the year. During this period, 314 wells started production, slightly below the count in the same time frame last year (353).

The chart above shows the almost 15-fold increase in oil production from horizontal wells in the Permian, in the last 6.5 years. Preliminary May production came in at just over 3.3 million bo/d, which will probably rise to close to 3.5 million bo/d once all revision data is in. Gas production from these wells is already over 11 Bcf/d (switch 'Product' to gas)

Oil production in North Dakota set a new record in June, at 1.425 million bo/d. In the first half of this year, 565 horizontal wells were brought online, 10% more than in the same period last year. Completion activity in June was high, with 135 wells put on production.

Oil production from horizontal wells in these states grew to close to 7 million bo/d in April (after upcoming revisions), setting a new record. That would represent a y-o-y growth of 1.3 million bo/d, but the rate at which production is increasing has slowed down since the end of last year.

---

- Published: 2019-08-08
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/niobrara-co-wy-update-through-april-2019/

These interactive presentations contain the latest oil & gas production data, from all 9,508 horizontal wells that started production in Colorado and Wyoming since 2009/2010, through October. Oil production in these 2 states set a new high in October, at just over 550 thousand bo/d. Gas production also came in at a record level, at close to 3 Bcf/d.

- Published: 2019-08-05
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-april-2019/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
April oil production came in at about 1.3 million bo/d, unchanged from a month earlier and just a few percent higher than a year ago. Natural gas production hovers still around 6 Bcf/d (switch 'Product' to 'gas'). Production from the ~2,500 horizontal wells that began operations in 2018 and in the first 4 months of this year (represented by the light and dark blue areas) contributed 54% of oil production, and 45% of gas production in April.

---

- Published: 2019-08-01
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-april-2019/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
With more revision data in, it is clear that a new record was set in March, at about 3.4 million bo/d. I expect that April production will eventually come in close to this level as well. Gas production is rising even more steeply, with over 11 Bcf/d produced in March (switch 'product' to gas to see this). Given that the gas/oil ratio rises over time for most of these wells, we expect that this trend will continue.

---

- Published: 2019-07-29
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/marcellus-pa-update-through-may-2019/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Similar to last year, May production dipped m-o-m and came in at 18.1 Bcf/d, a y-o-y increase of 2.4 Bcf/d. Well performance has increased a lot over the years, as is visible in the 'Well quality' tab. The 594 horizontal wells that began production in 2010 needed 6 years before they recovered an average of 3 Bcf of natural gas. The 748 horizontal wells that came online in 2017 recovered as much in just 14 months.

---

- Published: 2019-07-22
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-may-2019/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota stayed flat in April m-o-m, at 1.39 million bo/d, just below the record high in January (1.4 million bo/d). Natural gas production continued the trend upwards and reached 2.9 Bcf/d. After the slow winter months, 106 wells came online in April, slightly more than a year earlier (95)

- Published: 2019-07-04
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-march-2019/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production from horizontal wells in the Permian set another record in March, at over 3.2 million bo/d, even before upward revisions. As the blue areas indicate, in March more than 2/3rd of production came from wells that began production since 2018.Gas production also set a new record at close to 11 Bcf/d (switch "Product" to gas), although not all of it is wanted due to sometimes negative pricing.

---

- Published: 2019-07-01
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/marcellus-pa-update-through-april-2019/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
This interactive presentation contains the latest gas (and a little oil) production data, from all 8,915 horizontal wells in Pennsylvania that started producing from 2010 onward, through April. With March revisions in, it has become clear that another record was set, at 18.4 Bcf/d. April natural gas production was at a similar level. New wells recover on average more than 4 Bcf of gas in the first 2 years on production ('Well quality' tab). It took each of the ~1,300 horizontal wells, that began production in 2013, more than 5 years to reach that level.

---

- Published: 2019-06-25
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/fayetteville-update-through-march-2019/
- Categories: Arkansas
- Authors: Enno Peters
Not much shale oil & gas is produced in Arkansas nowadays. However, it is home to the Fayetteville shale gas basin, which produced in its heyday close to 3 Bcf/d of natural gas. In March this year this was down to 1.3 Bcf/d.

- Published: 2019-06-11
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-february-2019/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
These interactive presentations contain the latest oil & gas production data from 103,883 horizontal wells in 11 US states, through February 2019. A thousand horizontal wells were completed every month last year, on average. But now the frantic growth in oil production in the past 2 years appears to have stalled. Production in the first 2 months of this year was slightly down, although revisions will probably bring the level back up close to the record in December, at just over 6.7 million bo/d.

---

- Modified: 2026-03-05

February oil production came in at 1,22 million bo/d, the same rate of production as a year earlier. After revisions it will be a little higher, but still below the level at the end of last year. As is visible in the graph above, the contribution of wells that came online before 2018 was just about 50% in February.

---

- Published: 2019-06-03
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-february-2019/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
The breakneck growth in Permian production during the past 2 years appears to have taken a breather, at least temporarily. I expect that after revisions, production in February will come in 5-10% higher than shown here, or about 3.2-3.3 million bo/d. This would represent just a modest gain from the end of last year. Gas production is now above 10 Bcf/d.

Gas production in Pennsylvania dropped by a small amount in March, but remained close to January's record output, at 18.2 Bcf/d. In the first quarter of this year, 147 wells started production, almost unchanged from a year earlier (142).

Oil production in North Dakota rose by 4% in March m-o-m to 1.39 million bo/d, just below the record high in January (1.4 million bo/d). Natural gas production was even up by 6.5%, reaching 2.8 Bcf/d, a new all-time high.

- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
January production from these wells was at a similar level as a month earlier, with about 6.6 million bo/d (after revisions). The Permian has been responsible for most of the growth in the past 2 years. If you exclude this basin (using the "Basin" filter at the bottom), you will see that combined production in the other basins only surpassed the 2014 peak in December.

- Modified: 2026-03-05

- Categories: Haynesville
- Authors: Enno Peters
Gas production in the Haynesville rose by more than 2 Bcf/d in 2018 to well over 8 Bcf/d, which was the strongest growth since 2012, breaking the previous record set 6 years earlier.

- Published: 2019-05-02
- Modified: 2026-03-05

- Published: 2019-04-29
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/marcellus-pa-update-through-february-2019/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Gas production in Pennsylvania fell by 1% m-o-m in February to 18.1 Bcf/d, after setting a new record in January. Compared with a year earlier, this was just over 2 Bcf/d higher.

---

- Published: 2019-04-23
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-february-2019/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota fell in February by 5% m-o-m to 1.34 million bo/d. As is common in the winter months, few new wells started production (64), and more wells were shut-in. Gas production also saw a drop, but the gas oil ratio continued to rise; now 2 Mcf of natural gas is produced with every barrel of oil. Seven years ago this was only 1 Mcf per barrel.

---

- Published: 2019-04-17
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/niobrara-co-wy-update-through-january-2019/
- Categories: Niobrara
- Authors: Enno Peters
These interactive presentations contain the latest oil & gas production data, from all 10,287 horizontal wells that started production in Colorado and Wyoming since 2009/2010, through January. Oil production in these 2 states started the year at record production (after revisions), at over 620 thousand bo/d. Both states contributed to the growth in the past 12 months; Colorado with ~20%, and Wyoming even at almost 50% (although from a lower base). Production in the Powder River Basin has been mostly responsible for the latter, and is now at over 120 thousand bo/d.

---

- Published: 2019-04-08
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-december-2018/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
December production from these ~100 thousand horizontal wells was above 6.5 million bo/d, a y-o-y growth of 1.3 million bo/d (after revisions). This was a similar growth rate as a year earlier. Natural gas production increased to ~60 Bcf/d, growing by about 10 Bcf/d during the year, which also matched the growth in the previous year.

---

- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-december-2018/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
December oil production, close to 1.3 million bo/d, barely changed y-o-y. Just over 1,800 new horizontal wells were able to counter the ~45% decline in legacy production in 2018.

- Published: 2019-04-01
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-december-2018/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
December oil production came in at around 3.1 million bo/d (after revisions), 1 million bo/d higher than a year earlier. Close to 4,400 horizontal wells were completed in 2018, 23% more than in 2017. As is represented by the blue area in December 2018, about 2/3rd of December production came from wells that began production in 2018.

---

- Published: 2019-03-28
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/utah-update-through-january-2019/
- Categories: Utah
- Authors: Enno Peters
We've just included Utah in our coverage of horizontal drilling in the U.S., bringing the total number of covered states to 12. These 12 states are responsible for 99% of recent horizontal drilling activity based on the Baker Hughes horizontal rig count. Although the absolute amount of oil & gas produced in this state is relatively is low, compared with the other basins you've read about here, the growth rate has been pretty high in the past 2 years. Oil production from horizontal wells was 33 thousand bo/d in January this year, while it was only 14 thousand bo/d 2 years earlier.

---

- Published: 2019-03-25
- Modified: 2026-03-05

- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Gas production in Pennsylvania started this year at 18.3 Bcf/d, a new record and an annual growth rate of 2.5 Bcf/d (16%). In 2018 10% more wells began production compared with the year before and, as you can find in the 'Well quality' tab, they peaked at a 16% higher rate (11,900 Mcf/d versus 10,300 Mcf/d).

---

- Published: 2019-02-26
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/marcellus-pa-update-through-december-2018/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Gas production in Pennsylvania ended last year at over 18 Bcf/d, with a y-o-y growth rate of 2 Bcf/d. This was the result of the addition of 6.8 Bcf/d from the just over 800 horizontal wells that started production in 2018, minus the 4.8 Bcf/d decline from legacy wells. Such a large contribution from new wells (6.8 Bcf/d in a year) has not been seen before in Pennsylvania.

Oil production in North Dakota increased by almost 2% m-o-m to just over 1.4 million barrels of oil per day in December, after a small drop in November. In December 121 wells started production, vs. 98 in November.

- Published: 2019-01-21
- Modified: 2026-03-05

- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota dropped 1% m-o-m to 1,376 kbo/d in November, after a record output in October. The main factor behind this drop appears to be the smaller number of wells that went into production in October (119) and November (108), after a busy summer (~140 completions per month).

- Modified: 2026-03-05

- Published: 2019-01-07
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-september-2018/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
The graph above shows clearly the astonishing rise in oil production in the Permian in the past 2 years, as oil production from horizontal wells more than doubled to over 2.8 million bo/d in September (which will be visible after upcoming revisions). More than 1.5 million bo/d in September came from ~3,200 horizontal wells that started in 2018.

---

- Published: 2019-01-03
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-september-2018/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
Although from the above graph it appears that oil production in the Eagle Ford has moved sideways in 2018, due to the typical reporting lag in Texas, I expect to see that after revisions production from horizontal wells will come in at 1.3 - 1.4 million bo/d in September. That would be highest level in the past 2.5 years, but still well below the 2015 peak.

---

- Published: 2018-12-31
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/marcellus-pa-update-through-october-2018/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
New production records have been set in the 2nd half of every year since 2010, and 2018 was no different. Gas production in October from horizontal wells came in at 17.6 Bcf/d, about 20% higher than October 2017 (14.1 Bcf/d). The 687 wells that started production in the first 10 months of 2018 already contributed more than 1/3rd of total gas production in October (6 Bcf/d).

---

- Published: 2018-12-17
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-october-2018/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota climbed to 1,392 kbo/d in October, a month-on-month increase of more than 2%, and again a new record for the state. In the first 10 months this year 1,045 wells were brought online, which was more than in each of the two years before.

---

- Published: 2018-12-10
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-august-2018/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production from horizontal wells in these states grew by almost 2 million bo/d in the 2 years through August. This growth rate was similar as in the boom years of 2013-14. The Permian was responsible for most of this gain, which you'll see if you show the production data by 'Basin' (using the 'Show production by' selection).

---

- Published: 2018-12-04
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-august-2018/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in the Permian from horizontal wells has continued to rise at an astonishing pace, adding about 1 million bo/d in production capacity in the 12 months through August, to about 2.7 million bo/d (with upward revisions coming). The main driver behind this growth is the high level of completion activity; so far more than 2,800 horizontal wells have been completed this year, double the level of just 2 years ago, and 40% higher than last year.

---

- Published: 2018-11-29
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-august-2018/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
Since the low point two years ago, oil production in the Eagle Ford has kept growing. I expect that after revisions August production will eventually come in at around 1.3 million bo/d (~100 kbo/d higher than shown now). Natural gas production follows a very similar pattern. If you switch 'Product' to gas, you'll find that in 2018 total gas production was just below 6 Bcf/d.

---

- Published: 2018-11-26
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/niobrara-co-wy-update-through-september-2018/
- Categories: Niobrara
- Authors: Enno Peters
These interactive presentations contain the latest oil & gas production data, from all 9,508 horizontal wells that started production in Colorado and Wyoming since 2009/2010, through September. Since the last post, we've also added several other regions in these 2 states, and they are included here. In August a new record was set, at over 0.5 million bo/d. After revisions are in I believe September will show a higher level again. Weld County produces about 75% of this output (group production by 'County' to see this).

---

- Published: 2018-11-22
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/marcellus-pa-update-through-september-2018/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Gas production from horizontal wells came in higher again in September, at 17.4 Bcf/d. Output has grown by more than 10% in the 4 preceding months, driven mostly by an increase in well completions; In both August and September, 107 wells started production, the highest since the end of 2014.

---

- Published: 2018-11-20
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-september-2018/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota jumped to 1,359 kbo/d in September, a month-on-month increase of more than 5%, which again set a new record. Just over 150 wells were brought into production, the highest number in more than 3 years. The year-to-date number of new producers is now almost the same as for the full 2017 (933 vs. 992).

---

- Published: 2018-11-12
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-through-july-2018/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil and gas production from horizontal wells kept setting new records through the first 7 months of this year. The 5,600 new producers contributed ~2.2 million bo/d and 10.4 Bcf/d in July, versus 4,600 new producers in the same period last year (which contributed 1.6 million bo/d and 9.1 Bcf/d in July last year).

---

- Published: 2018-11-05
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-july-2018/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
In July 228 horizontal wells started production in the Eagle Ford, the highest number in more than 3 years. Although the graph above shows a dip in production in July, this is partially because of reporting lag, and I expect that when these wells have a full month on production in August total output will show a bump.

---

- Published: 2018-10-29
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-july-2018/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Output has continued to rise fast in the first half year, adding over 400 thousand barrels of oil per day, from horizontal wells. The apparent drop in July is as usual due to incomplete data. As the graph above shows, more than 75% of oil production in July came from the ~5.7 thousand wells that started since the beginning of 2017.

---

- Published: 2018-10-23
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/marcellus-pa-update-through-august-2018/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Gas production from horizontal wells in this state set another record in August, at 17 Bcf/d. An important factor behind this was a large number of wells that were brought online during the month; 108, the highest in almost 4 years. Almost 25% of total gas production in August came from just 265 wells, that each produced at a rate higher than 10 MMcf/d.

---

- Published: 2018-10-18
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-august-2018/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in North Dakota came in at 1,291 kbo/d in August, after a month-on-month rise of 1.7%, setting again a new record. As the graph shows, the 782 wells that started production in 2018 contributed already to more than 1/3rd of total production in August. They produced more than the ~10k wells that started before 2015.

---

- Published: 2017-12-20
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/marcellus-utica-update-through-september-2017/
- Categories: Appalachia
- Authors: Enno Peters
Gas production from these unconventional wells reached a new high in September, just below 20 Bcf/d. Initial productivity increased a lot over the past year. That explains why the 787 wells that started production in first 9 months of 2017 are in September at an average production rate of about 50% higher compared with the 733 wells that started in the same period last year.

- Published: 2017-11-09

- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
---

- Published: 2017-11-05
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-august-2017/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
As the graph shows, oil production in the Texas side of the Permian has grown steeply in the first half of 2017, faster than in any other 6 month period. In August, more than 70% of the total oil production was produced by wells that came online since 2016. The difference with my previous posts on the Permian is quite large, as a significant amount of production was not yet processed by the RRC (and thus had the status 'pending').

---

- Published: 2017-11-02
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-august-2017/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
In this "Ultimate Recovery" ( EUR) overview the relationship between production rates, and cumulative production is revealed. Longer laterals and larger proppant volumes have increased the initial production rates in recent years, but their impact on the ensuing decline seems limited.

---

---

- Published: 2017-09-18
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-through-july-2017/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
---

- Published: 2017-09-14
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-may-2017/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
As I now have access to some data regarding the status and performance of individual wells (especially regular "well tests", but also a number of other sources), it is now possible to show the production from almost all horizontal wells in this region. Only a very small number of wells are still missing (they are not yet assigned to a lease, or have no well tests). This improvement explains why the numbers in the total production overview above are now almost 100 kbo/d higher than earlier posts on the Permian.

---

- Published: 2017-09-12
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-may-2017/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
The "Productivity distribution" overview is handy if you're interested to know the % of wells that reach at least a certain amount of production, within a specified time frame. For example, using the black curve, you'll see that of all these wells that have produced at least 2 years, just over 10% has done more than 200 kbo. You'll get this number by subtracting the running total in the tooltip (89.63%) from 100%, at the 200 kbo mark. Of course you can use the selection boxes to narrow down the population of wells that you wish to look at.

---

- Published: 2017-08-23
- Modified: 2026-03-05

---

- Published: 2017-04-06
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-december-2016/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in the Permian has risen strongly in 2016. As you can see from the dark blue area, in December, about half of the oil production came from horizontal wells that started production in 2016.

---

- Published: 2017-04-04
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-through-december-2016/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production in this region kept falling in December, and came in just below 1050 kbo/d. As you can see from the light blue area, production from all wells that started before 2016 declined during 2016 from 1440 kbo/d to 675 kbo/d. This means that the overall annual decline rate was just above 50%. For gas this metric was a little less steep (just above 40%). Future revisions will increase the displayed production numbers for the last few months a bit.

---

- Published: 2017-02-02

- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters
Oil production declined further in October, albeit at a smaller pace than in the previous months. Fewer than 1200 horizontal wells were put on production in 2016, through October, compared with over 2600 a year earlier, and more than 3700 in 2014.

- Authors: Enno Peters
---

- Published: 2016-11-08
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-through-july-2016/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
a continuous improvement in average well productivity, since 2013. 2016 wells so far are on a cumulative level about twice that compared with the average 2013 well, by month 7 on production (80 vs 40 kbo). It will be very interesting to see how long these production rates can stay higher, compared with earlier wells.

- Categories: Appalachia

---

- Published: 2016-10-14
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/north-dakota-update-until-august-2016/
- Categories: North Dakota
- Authors: Enno Peters
---

- Published: 2016-10-06
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/us-update-until-june-2016/
- Categories: US
- Authors: Enno Peters
---

- Published: 2016-10-03
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/permian-update-until-june-2016/
- Categories: Permian
- Authors: Enno Peters
The EIA reports a much larger amount of oil production from the Permian in its Drilling Productivity report, than I do here. The reason for this difference is that the EIA also counts production from a significant number of vertical wells there (many of which are fracked), while I only report horizontal wells. I only report these, to be able to make better apple-to-apple comparisons possible with horizontal well productivity in other basins.

---

- Published: 2016-09-30
- Modified: 2026-03-05
- URL: https://novilabs.com/blog/eagle-ford-update-until-june-2016/
- Categories: Eagle Ford
- Authors: Enno Peters

- Published: 2026-04-29
- Modified: 2026-04-29
- URL: https://novilabs.com/news/novi-labs-acquires-turing-analytics-to-establish-a-unified-north-american-platform-for-upstream-investment-decisions/
- News categories: Company
- Authors: Maria Pesantez
Acquisition brings together U.S. and Canadian upstream analytical datasets to support capital allocation decisions for operators and energy investors

---

- Published: 2026-04-15
- Modified: 2026-05-20
- URL: https://novilabs.com/news/novi-attends-urtec-2026/
- News categories: Exhibition / Event
- Authors: Maria Pesantez
Novi Labs is heading to the Unconventional Resources Technology Conference (URTeC) 2026, June 22–24 in Houston. Visit booth #919 to meet our Product, Engineering, and Research teams all in one place, and explore upstream research, well-level analytics, and ML-powered production forecasting, plus the latest product innovations across the Novi platform. We’ll also present our latest reservoir engineering findings, highlighting the application of AI in this year’s accepted URTeC technical papers.
